name: batch-size clock ladder (Attempt 1A)
question: Is the lag-kernel memory clock the optimizer-noise clock dSigma = eta*sqrt(B)*dt (relaxation rate scaling with batch size via s ~ B^-1/2), or B-blind (pure eta/step clock)?

design.model: m config d=384 nh=6 nl=6 block=256, AdamW (0.9,0.95) wd=0.1 eps=1e-8 clip=1.0
design.trunk: seed 1337, bs=48, linear warmup 400 -> peak 1.5e-3, constant to step 3000; checkpoint (model+opt+torch gen state) must pass 200-step bitwise replay before any arm is trusted (G3)
design.eval: eval-bs=48 fixed, eval_gen=12345 (G2); dense every 4 steps in [2900,4600] ([2900,6200] for B2=12), n_eval=10; tail n_eval=40 over last 1000 stage-2 steps

analysis.per_arm_fit: r(t) = F_inf + A*exp(-(t-3000)/tau), r2 >= 0.6 gate (analyze_floor protocol)
analysis.regression: log tau = a + b_B*log B2 + b_eta*log eta2
analysis.paired_floor_gap: gap = G(eta2) * B2^q using drop-minus-control pairs at same (seed,B2), bitwise-shared batch sequences

verdicts.C2_noise_clock: b_B = -0.5 +/- 0.15 with 90% CI excluding 0; independent amplitude check q in [-0.65, -0.35]

continue_close.noise_clock: CONTINUE major: generalized clock real; re-derive deployment kernel with B-corrected clock; feeds reopen item (e)
continue_close.B_blind: CONTINUE one round on the other branch: 10.7M slow mode is not optimizer-noise relaxation; amplitude(B^-1/2 floors)-vs-timescale(flat tau) decomposition itself reportable; mechanism hunt moves to Attempt 3 p>1 bed
continue_close.unmeasurable: CLOSE the B-axis at 10.7M; reopen contingent on the 25M bed

dof_note: zero new formula DOF; both target exponents forced by s ~ B^-1/2
